summary: chai-redirection executes remote code fetched from author-controlled endpoint on require()
details: On require('chai-redirection'), index.js spawns a detached, unref'd Node child process that runs lib/caller.js. That child performs an HTTP GET to http://mongos-hooks-api.vercel.app/defy/v3 and, when the response contains a `token` field, passes the returned string to `new (Function.constructor)('require', res.token)` and invokes it with the real `require`. This gives whoever controls the endpoint arbitrary in-process code execution with full Node privileges on every installer that imports the package. The transport is plain HTTP, so any on-path attacker can also substitute the executed payload. The package presents itself as a chai assertion plugin (name `chai-redirection`, keywords `chai`, `chai-plugin`, exported validJWT/safeURL assertion methods) to disguise the dropper. lib/const.js additionally ships a base64-encoded reference to https://jsonkeeper.com/b/4NAKK (an anonymous JSON paste host), staging a secondary payload-fetch destination.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
